recent_posts.text:
A Framework for Frontier AI and the Dawning of a New Age
> Demis Hassabis argues AGI could be one of humanity's most transformative technologies.
> “The magnitude of this technology’s impact will be unprecedented, perhaps 10x of the Industrial Revolution at 10x the speed.”
> However, he warns that accelerating frontier AI requires stronger safeguards, international coordination, and responsible deployment to manage cyber, bio, and other emerging risks.
> He proposes a U.S-led Frontier AI Standards Body to evaluate advanced models, set evolving safety benchmarks, audit frontier labs, and establish a global framework for safe AGI development while preserving innovation.
Source:
https://x.com/demishassabis/status/2076957440109625718

Clarity Act enters critical legislative window –
Link
The U.S. Senate's CLARITY Act entered a crucial four-week window that will decide whether it can pass before Congress adjourns in August. President Trump called for Senate passage, citing competition with China on crypto and AI.
Matters because:
The bill could improve regulatory certainty for U.S. digital asset markets if it advances in time.

tweet.text: Critical week for Clarity, which also happens to be the one-year anniversary of GENIUS. A reminder of the incredible amount of hard work that has gone into this bill, but also of the time we’ve already lost. We cannot afford to delay any longer.

U.S.-Iran tensions amplify oil and inflation pressure –
Link
Escalating U.S.-Iran hostilities triggered reciprocal airstrikes and pushed Brent crude futures more than 3% higher to nearly $79 per barrel. Markets are watching for Strait of Hormuz disruptions that could lift energy prices, keep inflation sticky, and weigh on crypto risk assets.
Matters because:
Energy shocks can feed inflation and make Fed easing harder, which is typically negative for crypto.

recent_posts.text:
data refineries: turning abundant data into trustworthy, actionable intelligence
this is the moat
https://x.com/michellelsun/status/2076705184898785637

tweet.text:
Data volume is no longer the bottleneck for robotics training. 

It’s building dynamic pipelines - like oil refineries in the AI age - that guarantee trustworthy lineage + usable signal for training and deployment.

A single robot spits out terabytes of raw camera, LiDAR & IMU streams daily. 

Training data supply is exploding globally, especially with the surge of data collection startups across India, China, and beyond.

In order for these data to be useful, we need robust processing pipelines that handle : 
- Provenance tracking (where did this frame come from? which embodiment? under what conditions?)
- Cleaning, synchronization, labeling
- Repeatedly & reliably turning it into high-quality multi-modal tokens for training and real-time deployment

The new moat in robotics is the robust, dynamic data infrastructure that turns raw observations into trustworthy, actionable intelligence.

Who’s building the best data refineries?

recent_posts.text: https://x.com/_clarktang/status/2076750785304551917

tweet.text:
I think there is general confusion around how AI works, AI tokenomics, and ultimately *what is actually priced in* for the AI trade - and that some of the existing arguments are at odds with one another

Firstly to clear this up - what Brad and Gavin are saying are completely in agreement, what Gavin is laying out here is the *mega bull case* as he so states in the first sentence of his tweet lol

The base case we are all living with is that the labs are going to continue to generate a significant amount of revenue this year and next year. OpenAI was already the fastest growing company of all time (and still is)... but Anthropic has just grown *SO* fast that OpenAI's growth look slow by comparison

The basic chain for all of this together is as follows:

Power (generation, interconnect, regulation) ->
DC Shell (construction, equipment, regulation) -> 
Semiconductors (compute, memory, interconnect, adv packaging, wafer capacity) ->
Hardware (networking, storage) ->
Software (data, infra

tweet.text:
The Physical AI data space is exploding, but most vendors still share samples through Google Drive or Hugging Face links that are slow and painful to evaluate.

We built the Human Archive Catalog so researchers can quickly browse and filter our off-the-shelf datasets by environment, hardware configuration, modality, annotations, and more.

If you’re a robotics or world model researcher, request access below:

https://catalog.humanarchive-qa.ai/request-access

Treasury yields spike on Iran tensions and rate hike expectations –
Link
U.S. two-year Treasury yields climbed to 4.24%, the highest since early 2025, as renewed Iran tensions pushed oil prices higher and increased speculation of Fed rate hikes. Market pricing now reflects nearly full expectation of a September hike, up from 66% a week ago.
Matters because:
Higher yields and tighter rate expectations can put pressure on risk assets and weigh on crypto valuations if inflation concerns stick.

Tempo Introduces Receive Policies
> Receive policies on Tempo let users decide which tokens and senders their accounts accept, and recover anything they don’t.
> It configures the assets an account accepts, the addresses it will accept transactions from, and who can reclaim a transfer that gets blocked
Source:
https://x.com/tempo/status/2076665200611311997

tweet.text:
Introducing Receive Policies

On most blockchains, any token can be sent to any account: There's no easy way to restrict who can send you assets or refuse an unwanted token.

Receive policies bring these features to Tempo, enforced by the protocol.

recent_posts.text:
Claude’s Values Across Models and Languages
How the values Claude expresses vary between Claude models and across languages.
> Anthropic developed a value axis method to quantify how Claude's expressed values shift across models and languages, compressing 3,000+ identified values into four dimensions: Deference vs. Caution, Warmth vs. Rigor, Depth vs. Brevity, and Candor vs. Execution.
> Findings show Sonnet 4.6 leans warm/deferential while Opus 4.7 leans cautious/rigorous, and Claude expresses more warmth in Arabic/Hindi versus more rigor and caution in English/Russian
Source:
https://www.anthropic.com/research/claude-values-models-languages

SABLIER LABS ENTERS MAINTENANCE MODE UNTIL JUNE 2028, HALTING NEW DEVELOPMENT WHILE KEEPING EXISTING STREAMS, VESTINGS, AND THE ONCHAIN PROTOCOL RUNNING INDEFINITELY
SOURCE

How to break the memory supercycle? Or is it even possible?
The Big 4 DRAM makers are projected to grow capacity from ~2M WPM in 2026 to 4.8M WPM by 2030 through major fab expansions
AI-driven demand is forecast to reach 157.5 EB by 2030. After accounting for HBM’s 4x wafer multiplier and node/yield factors, this creates a ~25% shortfall in general DRAM supply, keeping ASPs high (DRAM $1.5–2.0/Gb, HBM $5–6/Gb) and pushing the total DRAM market to ~$3 trillion
